features.title: Visual element inspector
features.description: Click any element in your running app to see its component, file, line, and computed styles. No DevTools spelunking.

features.title: Live style editor
features.description: Tweak padding, color, and typography in the browser. Annotask records the diff and turns it into a structured task your agent can apply.

features.title: Pin notes & arrows
features.description: Drop pins, draw arrows, and highlight regions on your live UI. Each annotation becomes a task with element context attached.

features.title: Section requests
features.description: Draw a section anywhere on the page and write what should go there. Your agent gets a placement-aware request, not a guess.

features.title: MCP server built in
features.description: Every annotask install ships an MCP server. Claude, Cursor, and any MCP-compatible agent can list, lock, and resolve tasks.

features.title: Round-trip review
features.description: Agents post resolutions back to annotask. You accept, deny, or ask follow-up questions without leaving the browser.

features.title: Vue, React, Svelte, Astro
features.description: First-class transform support across major frameworks. Same UX everywhere, framework-aware element labels.

features.title: Vite & Webpack
features.description: Drop-in plugins for both bundlers. Zero config in the common case.

features.title: MFE-aware
features.description: Annotations route to the correct micro-frontend so a host app and embedded children stay sane during reviews.

features.title: A11y scanner
features.description: Bundled axe-core scan with one-click fix tasks. Keyboard, contrast, semantics — all surfaced inline.

features.title: Performance monitor
features.description: Web Vitals, bundle analysis, interaction timing. Perf regressions become tasks the same way design changes do.

features.title: Error capture
features.description: Console errors and warnings deduplicate, group, and convert into fix tasks with full stack context.

testimonials.name: Maya Chen
testimonials.quote: We replaced four tools with annotask. Designers comment on the live app, my agent applies it overnight, and we wake up to a clean PR.

testimonials.name: Diego Romero
testimonials.quote: The MCP integration is the cheat code. Our coding agent picks up tasks straight from the browser annotations.

testimonials.name: Priya Subramanian
testimonials.quote: Annotask cut our design QA cycle from a week to a day. The element context it captures is uncanny.

testimonials.name: Tomás Werner
testimonials.quote: I love that it lives next to the dev server. No SaaS, no logins, no shipping screenshots over Slack.

testimonials.name: Aïsha Bell
testimonials.quote: The style editor lets me actually try the change before describing it. The agent reads my edits, not my prose.

testimonials.name: Henry Okafor
testimonials.quote: We onboarded annotask in an afternoon. Two weeks later it's the only tool everyone agrees we shouldn't remove.

pricing.name: Solo
features: Local-first, runs on your dev server

changelog.headline: Richer compact task summaries
highlights: Type-specific context surfaced in summary view
Smaller default payloads for MCP listings

changelog.headline: Page-wide perf & a11y filtering
highlights: Fixed mfe filter dropping page-wide perf/error/a11y tasks
Astro and htmx now marked experimental in package metadata

changelog.headline: Syntax-highlighted JSON view
highlights: JSON view in task detail is now syntax highlighted
annotask-apply skill now uses npx prefix in CLI commands

changelog.headline: Shell theme system v2
highlights: Custom theme editor with live preview

changelog.headline: MCP Streamable HTTP
highlights: MCP server now uses Streamable HTTP transport

changelog.headline: Module Federation support
highlights: MFE child mode for embedded apps
Annotations route to the correct child
